reasoning:
TSLA entered 2025 near its all-time highs (around $400-$480 depending on the exact peak in Dec 2024 / early Jan) and has swiftly crashed throughout the first quarter. By April 7, 2025, TSLA plunged to an intraday low of $217.41 on broader market selloffs and poor Q1 sales data, marking a nearly 44% YTD drop. The stock then saw a huge 23% bounce on April 9 after the announcement of transient tariff pauses, bringing its price likely back into the $260-280 range. 

The question asks whether TSLA stock will drop below $200 a share at any point in 2025. Given that we are barely four months into the year, and the stock already breached $220 amidst rapidly deteriorating fundamental narratives (year-over-year sales drops and high volatility tied to political/China events), a drop of another ~15%-25% from its current price to hit $199.99 before December 31 seems highly probable. High-beta stocks like TSLA routinely experience large swings, and it has already tested support levels near $215. While a sudden macroeconomic or political reprieve could keep it buoyed, the structural issues heading into the remainder of 2025 give it plenty of time and momentum to cross the $200 mark. The probability is set at 70%.
